title: Mandatory Publishing, Returns, Alternate Exchanges, and Route Audits · Boundary Failure Triage

objectives: Register return handling before mandatory publication and correlate returned content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.

content.text: A debug messaging team handles case 3588 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep mandatory return, alternate custody, confirmation, and route uncertainty separate; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Alternate Return Tradeoff, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Alternate exchanges provide the publisher the same immediate route-failure evidence as mandatory returns.
